schema_notes: NoCentral AP capability matrix. Data extracted verbatim from the HPE Aruba Networking WLAN Platforms Software Support Matrix, Instant 8.x columns only. Bump schema_version only when the shape of this file changes (new fields, renamed fields, etc.). Do NOT bump it for content-only refreshes from HPE — those bump matrix_revision and matrix_data_date instead.

matrix_source.source_url_note: Pulled directly from the HPE support portal by an SE. Document updates ad-hoc — no fixed release cadence. Jim's workflow: refresh PDF from portal, compare Rev number and data date, regenerate this file, commit.

instant_8x_context.notes: HPE has announced 8.13 as the final Instant 8.x release. Every TBD max in this file will ultimately resolve to 8.13 (or earlier if HPE drops a platform mid-train). There is no 8.14 and there will not be. Features, bug fixes, and CVE patches beyond 8.13 land in AOS-10 only, which requires HPE Aruba Networking Central and is outside NoCentral's scope.

nocentral_hardware_scope.notes: NoCentral supports AP-3xx through AP-6xx hardware only. Older models (2xx and earlier) are not applicable. Models outside this range should not appear in the matrix below — if the VC reports one, the Planner should treat it as out-of-scope rather than unsupported.

tbd_semantics.meaning: Max version not yet decided by HPE. Literal — HPE may announce a cap at any point.
tbd_semantics.planner_interpretation: For any train currently in existence (up to and including the instant_8x_final_release), TBD passes the max check. UI should display 'currently uncapped' rather than implying open-ended future support. Combine with instant_8x_context.final_release to show the effective ceiling: e.g., AP-505 max = TBD, effective ceiling = 8.13.

unknown_model_handling.guidance: If the VC reports an AP model not present in this file AND within nocentral_hardware_scope, flag it as 'unknown — matrix update needed'. This is the canary signal that HPE has shipped a new AP and Jim needs to refresh the matrix. Do not assume sensible defaults — unknown means unknown.

aos10_only_models_note: Models listed on page 5 of the source matrix with N/A for Instant 8.x (AP-503R, AP-605R, AP-674, AP-725, AP-734, AP-735, AP-745, AP-754, AP-755, AP-763, AP-764, AP-765) are AOS-10-only hardware and cannot run Instant firmware. They are DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from this file. If such a model appears in a VC cluster report, that is a hardware/licensing mismatch, not a version mismatch. The Planner should detect this case separately (by checking an out-of-band AOS-10-only model list) and surface a distinct message — remediation is platform migration to Central, not firmware upgrade or hardware replacement.
